name: rten-convert
documentation:
# rten-convert

rten-convert converts ONNX models to `.rten` format, for use with the
[RTen](https://github.com/robertknight/rten) machine learning runtime.

## Installation

The conversion tool requires Python >= 3.10. To install the tool, run:

```sh
pip install rten-convert
```

## Usage

```sh
rten-convert your-model.onnx your-model.rten
```

The second argument is optional. If omitted the output filename will be the
input filename with the `.onnx` extension replaced with `.rten`.

## Versioning

The `rten-convert` tool and `rten` library use common version numbering. A
model produced by `rten-convert` version X can be executed by `rten` version X
or newer.

## Development

To install this tool from a checkout of the Git repository, run:

```sh
pip install -e .
```

After making changes, run the QA checks. First, install the development
dependencies:

```
pip install -r requirements.dev.txt
```

Then run:

```
make check
```

functions.name: rten_convert.util.round_up
documentation: Round up `value` to the next multiple of `base`.

functions.name: rten_convert.util.write_padding
documentation:
Write `n` bytes of zero padding at the end of a file.

```
param max_padding

Maximum value for . This is a sanity check to catch unexpectedly
large padding sizes.
```

methods.name: rten_convert.tensor_data.TensorDataBuilder.add_tensor
documentation:
Add a tensor to be written to the tensor data segment.

Returns the offset that the data will be stored at, relative to the
start of the tensor data segment.

methods.name: rten_convert.tensor_data.TensorDataBuilder.write
documentation: Write out tensor data to a file.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.tensor_data.TensorDataBuilder.offset
documentation: End offset of written data from start of tensor data.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.tensor_data.TensorDataBuilder.align
documentation: Alignment of each tensor's data, relative to the start of the tensor data.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.warn_once
documentation:
Emit a warning if not already emitted.

This is used to reduce output noise if the same problem arises many times
when converting a model.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.snake_case_to_pascal_case
documentation: Transform a snake_case string to PascalCase.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.check_ints_length
documentation:
Check that an ints attribute has a fixed length.

Various ONNX operators allow for a wider range of dimensions and per-axis
values (eg. for strides, dilations, padding...) than this library currently
supports.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.read_pads
documentation:
Update the padding attributes for an operator.

Reads padding attributes from an ONNX operator and updates the attributes
for an RTen operator.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.read_strides
documentation: Read a stride specification from an ONNX operator.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.read_dilations
documentation: Read a dilation specification from an ONNX operator.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.convert_data_type
documentation:
Convert a data type enum value from ONNX to RTen.

```
param onnx_dtype

Data type from .

return

Value from 
```

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.op_node_from_onnx_operator
documentation:
Map an ONNX operator to the equivalent operator in this library.

See <https://github.com/onnx/onnx/blob/main/docs/Operators.md> for list of
available ONNX operators and attributes for each.

```
param onnx_op

ONNX operator to convert

param node_index_from_name

Mapping of constant and value tensor node names
in the graph to corresponding input names

param constant_nodes

Map of constant value tensor node names

param add_node

Function that adds a new node to the graph and returns its
node ID. This is called if an operator attribute needs to be converted
to a constant input.
```

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.duplicate_node_names
documentation:
Check for node names which are duplicated in `nodes` and return the
duplicates.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.graph_from_onnx_graph
documentation:
Parse an ONNX model into a graph representation compatible with this library.

```
param allow_captures

Whether operator inputs are allowed to reference value names that do
not appear in the graph. If true, such inputs are captured from the
parent scope at runtime.
```

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.build_constant_node
documentation: Serialize a constant tensor value (eg. model weights) into a FlatBuffers model.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.write_vec
documentation:
Serialize a list into a vector in a FlatBuffers buffer.

`start_vec` is the generated function that starts the vector.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.build_operator_node
documentation: Serialize an operator into a FlatBuffers model.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.build_value_node
documentation: Serialize a placeholder for an input/output value into a FlatBuffers model.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.build_metadata
documentation: Serialize model metadata into a flatbuffers model.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.build_graph
documentation: Serialize a computation graph into a flatbuffers model.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.serialize_model
documentation:
Serialize a model into a flatbuffers model.

This serializes the parsed graph representation into the flatbuffers-based
model format that this library uses.

```
param graph

The main graph for the model

param metadata

Model metadata

param tensor_data

Object that will be used to write the tensor data in a separate segment
of the file that follows the model buffer.
```

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.write_header
documentation:
Write the model file header.

```
param model_data_offset

Offset of the FlatBuffers data for the model, relative to the start of the file.

param model_data_len

Length of the FlatBuffers data for the model.

param tensor_data_offset

Offset of the tensor data segment, relative to the start of the file.
```

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.sha256
documentation: Generate SHA-256 hash of a file as a hex string.

functions.name: rten_convert.converter.generate_metadata
documentation:
Generate metadata to embed into RTen model.

```
param onnx_path

Path to .onnx file

param metadata_path

Path to JSON file containing additional metadata
```

variables.name: rten_convert.converter.METADATA_BUILDER_FNS
documentation: Map of metadata field to function that serializes this field.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.Node
documentation: Base class for all graph nodes (constants, values, operators).

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.ConstantNode
documentation:
Data for a constant graph node.

These are used for model weights, biases etc.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.OperatorNode

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.OperatorNode.attrs
documentation:
Attributes object or None.

This should be the operator-specific attributes object generated by flatc.
eg. `sg.AveragePoolAttrsT` for the AveragePool op.
Data for an operator graph node.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.ValueNode

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ValueNode.__init__
documentation:
Initialize a value node.

```
param name

Unique name of the value

param shape

Expected shape of tensor at runtime

param dtype

Expected data type of tensor at runtime. Value from .
```

Data for a value placeholder graph node.

These are used for operator inputs and outputs.

The shape can be missing, or a mix of fixed and symbolic (unknown at model
export time) sizes.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.Graph.inputs
documentation: Indices of nodes in `nodes` that are model inputs.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.Graph.outputs
documentation: Indices of nodes in `nodes` that are model outputs.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.Graph.captures
documentation: Indices of nodes in `nodes` that are captured from parent scopes at runtime.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.Metadata
documentation:
Model metadata.

This corresponds to the `ModelMetadata` struct in RTen. See its docs for
details of the individual fields.

When adding new fields here, they also need to be added to
`METADATA_BUILDER_FNS`.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.get_attr
documentation: Get the value of an optional operator attribute.

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.get_bool_attr
documentation:
Get the value of an optional boolean operator attribute.

ONNX represents boolean attributes as "int" fields with values 0 or 1
rather than a dedicated boolean type. This method converts these
attributes to Python booleans.

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.get_enum_attr
documentation:
Get an optional attribute whose value is an enum variant.

The variant name is Pascal-Cased and looked up on the enum object.
eg. `round_prefer_floor` => `RoundPreferFloor`. If the Pascal-Cased
name matches a Python keyword, it is expected to be escaped, eg.
`none` => `None_`.

If the attribute value does not match any enum value, this will raise if
`fallback` is not specified, or emit a warning and use the value
`fallback` otherwise. Use of `fallback` is appropriate if the
substitution is unlikely to affect the resulting model's ability to run,
but might impact accuracy modestly.

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.ignore_attr
documentation:
Mark an attribute as ignored.

This is useful in cases where an attribute contains redundant information.

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.require_attr
documentation: Get the value of a required operator attribute.

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.generate_input_from_attr
documentation:
Generate a constant operator input from an attribute, if it exists.

Some operator inputs changed from attributes to inputs in different ONNX
releases. This function checks to see if an operator has an attribute
and synthesizes a constant input.

```
param input_index

Index of the input that the attribute corresponds to

param attr_name

Name of the attribute

param attr_type

Expected type of the attribute
```

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.check_attr
documentation:
Check if an operator has an unsupported non-default value for an attribute.

If `default` is a tuple, it specifies a set of acceptable defaults.

```
param name

The name of the operator attribute

param default

The value which is equivalent to the default behavior

param on_mismatch

Whether a mismatch should be treated as a fatal error in model
conversion or merely warn that this might cause a problem.
```

methods.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.unhandled_attrs
documentation: Return a list of attributes which have not been read.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.add_node
documentation:
Function that adds a new node to the graph and returns its ID.

This is used if a new constant node has to be generated to replace an
operator attribute.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader.input_indexes
documentation:
IDs of the operator's input nodes.

New inputs may be generated while reading an operator if it has an attribute
that needs to be converted to a dynamic input.

class_variables.name: rten_convert.converter.ONNXOperatorReader._handled_attrs
documentation: Names of attributes that have been handled.

Utiliy for extracting attribute and input values from an ONNX operator.

This keeps track of which attributes have been read so that we can warn about
any unhandled ones.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.PadAttrs
documentation: Common fields for RTen operator attributes which support padding.

classes.name: rten_convert.converter.DummyGraphT
documentation: Replacement for `sg.GraphT` whose `Pack` method serializes a `Graph` object.
